questions.q: In the Puranas, to which class of beings does the mighty Hiranyaksha belong?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha is a Daitya, a member of the asura race born of Diti, as related in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu slays the asura Hiranyaksha in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Vishnu descended as Varaha, the boar avatar, to defeat Hiranyaksha and rescue the earth.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, what great offence did Hiranyaksha commit against the earth?
options: He stole the sun from the sky
He dragged the earth-goddess down beneath the cosmic ocean
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha seized the earth-goddess Bhumi and dragged her down into the waters of the cosmic ocean.

questions.q: Which earth-goddess does Hiranyaksha drag into the cosmic ocean in the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha drags down Bhumi, the goddess of the earth, prompting Vishnu's rescue as Varaha.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which fellow Daitya is the brother of Hiranyaksha, born to the same parents?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu are brothers, both sons of the sage Kashyapa and his wife Diti.

questions.q: In the form of Varaha, how does Vishnu lift the earth out of the ocean after defeating Hiranyaksha?
questions.explanation: As the boar Varaha, Vishnu raises the earth upon his tusks from the depths of the cosmic ocean.

questions.q: The episode of Hiranyaksha and the boar Varaha is chiefly narrated in which text?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana gives the principal account of Hiranyaksha and his defeat by Varaha.

questions.q: What does the name 'Hiranyaksha' most nearly mean in Sanskrit?
questions.explanation: The name Hiranyaksha derives from 'hiranya' (gold) and 'aksha' (eye), meaning 'golden-eyed'.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the duel between Hiranyaksha and Varaha is said to take place chiefly where?
options: Beneath the waters of the cosmic ocean
On the peak of Mount Meru
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha and Varaha battle within the depths of the cosmic ocean where the earth had been submerged.

questions.q: The defeat of Hiranyaksha by Varaha primarily serves to accomplish which purpose in the Puranas?
options: The creation of the first humans
The rescue and restoration of the earth
The churning of the ocean of milk
The granting of immortality to the Devas
questions.explanation: Varaha's victory over Hiranyaksha restores the earth-goddess to her proper place in the cosmos.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, who are the parents of Hiranyaksha?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha was born to the sage Kashyapa and his wife Diti, mother of the Daityas.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu are said to be incarnations of which gatekeepers of Vishnu, cursed to be born as asuras?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that the doorkeepers Jaya and Vijaya, cursed by the sages, were born first as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Which sages, the four Kumaras, are said to have cursed Vishnu's gatekeepers, leading to Hiranyaksha's birth as a Daitya?
options: The Sanaka Kumaras (Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanatana, Sanatkumara)
questions.explanation: The four boy-sages Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanatana and Sanatkumara cursed Jaya and Vijaya, who were then born as Hiranyaksha and his brother.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, roughly how long is the duel between Hiranyaksha and Varaha said to last?
options: A full age (yuga) of the world
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes a mighty battle between Hiranyaksha and Varaha lasting a thousand years.

questions.q: Whose womb does the Bhagavata Purana say Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were conceived in during an inauspicious twilight hour?
questions.explanation: Diti conceived the twin Daityas at an unfavourable hour, and they were born as the powerful brothers Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Varaha, the avatar who slays Hiranyaksha, is counted among which famous group of Vishnu's incarnations?
questions.explanation: Varaha is the third of the Dashavatara, the ten principal incarnations of Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Puranic accounts, with what weapon does Hiranyaksha challenge and strike at Varaha during their combat?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes Hiranyaksha wielding a great mace against Varaha in their underwater duel.

questions.q: After Hiranyaksha's defeat, the asura brothers' line continues through Hiranyakashipu, whose devout son is whom?
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu's son Prahlada was a great devotee of Vishnu, the same Vishnu who had slain his uncle Hiranyaksha.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which deity becomes aware of the earth's plight after Hiranyaksha drags it down, and from whom the rescuing boar emerges?
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavata Purana, it is Brahma the creator who perceives the earth's submersion, and the boar Varaha emerges from his nostril.

questions.q: From whose nostril does the tiny boar that becomes the mighty Varaha first emerge, before confronting Hiranyaksha?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that a small boar issued from Brahma's nostril and swiftly grew into the colossal Varaha.

questions.q: The card describes Hiranyaksha as 'He Who Sank The Earth.' In the Puranas, into what does he sink the earth?
options: The underworld city of the Nagas
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha dragged the earth-goddess down into the cosmic ocean, the primeval waters from which Varaha lifted her.

questions.q: In Vaishnava tradition, the slaying of Hiranyaksha by Varaha is understood chiefly as an instance of what?
options: A contest of dance among the Devas
Vishnu descending to restore cosmic order (dharma)
The founding of a royal dynasty
questions.explanation: Varaha's defeat of Hiranyaksha exemplifies Vishnu's descent to protect dharma and restore the world's balance.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which lord of the sea does Hiranyaksha confront and humble in his arrogance before the coming of Varaha?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha challenged Varuna, lord of the waters, who counselled him to seek out Vishnu instead, foreshadowing Varaha.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, to whom does the defeated Varuna direct the boastful Hiranyaksha to find a worthy opponent?
questions.explanation: Varuna tells Hiranyaksha that only Vishnu can match him, sending the asura toward the encounter with Varaha.

questions.q: Which sage narrates the story of Hiranyaksha and Varaha to King Parikshit within the frame of the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana is recited by the sage Shukadeva (Shuka) to King Parikshit, including the tale of Hiranyaksha.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, after his cosmic conquests Hiranyaksha is described as roaming the worlds carrying which weapon, eager for battle?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha is portrayed wandering the three worlds with his mace upon his shoulder, seeking a worthy foe.

questions.q: The Daitya brothers Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu are slain, respectively, by which consecutive pair of Vishnu's avatars in the Dashavatara?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha falls to Varaha (the boar) and Hiranyakashipu to Narasimha (the man-lion), consecutive avatars in the Dashavatara.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana account, how does Varaha ultimately slay Hiranyaksha at the close of their duel?
options: With a blow of his fist (or paw), striking him down
questions.explanation: After their long combat, Varaha strikes Hiranyaksha a fatal blow with his hand, ending the asura's life.

questions.q: Through their father Kashyapa, the asura Hiranyaksha is kin to the Devas, who are born of which co-wife of Kashyapa?
questions.explanation: Aditi, mother of the Adityas (Devas), is co-wife with Diti, mother of Hiranyaksha, making Devas and Daityas half-brothers.

questions.q: In Vaishnava teaching, the apparent enmity of Hiranyaksha toward Vishnu is sometimes interpreted as what, given his origin as the cursed Jaya?
options: A sign that he was never truly an asura
A path of liberation through constant remembrance of the Lord, even in hostility
Proof that Vishnu cannot be opposed at all
Evidence that the curse was never fulfilled
questions.explanation: Some Vaishnava commentators hold that Jaya, born as Hiranyaksha, is said to have reached the Lord through unceasing thought of Vishnu, even in the form of enmity.
